results.rendered_html:
abhi-√pad to come near or towards, approach;
to approach (a deity) for imploring her help,  Śiś. ix, 27 ;
to come up (as an auxiliary), assist;
to seize, catch, overpower, master,  RV. x, 71, 9 ;  ŚBr.  &c.;
to take possession of  Mn. i, 30 , &c.;
to accept,  R. ;
to undertake, devote one's self to (acc.)

results.rendered_html:
abhipad 4 A.
1 To go to, draw near, approach (with acc.); rāvaṇāvarajā tatra rāghavaṃ madanāturā . abhipede nidāghārtā vyālīva malayadrumaṃ ..  R. 12. 32 ; 19. 11 ;
(pb)  Dk. 166 ;  K. 265 ; to enter (into)  Śi. 3. 25 ; sometimes with loc. also.
2 To look upon, consider, regard; to take or know to be; kṣaṇamabhyapadyata janairna mṛṣā gaganaṃ gaṇādhipatimūrtiriti  Śi. 9. 27. 
3 To help, assist; mayābhipannaṃ taṃ cāpi na sarpo dharṣayiṣyati  Mb. 
4 To seize, catch hold of; overpower, attack, subdue, take possession of, overcome, afflict; sarvataścābhipannaiṣā dhārtarāṣṭrī mahācamūḥ, caṃḍavātābhipannānāmudadhīnāmiva svanaḥ  Mb. ; see abhipanna also.
5 (a) To take, assume;  Ms. 1. 30.  (b) To accept, receive; nirāsvādyatamaṃ śūnyaṃ (rājyaṃ) bharato nābhipatsyate  Rām. 
6 To apply or devote oneself to, undertake, fall to, observe; sa ciṃtāmabhyapadyata  Rām. 
7 To honour.
